title: ggsunburstR
text: ggsunburstR R package creating sunburst icicle adjacency diagrams using ggplot2. accepts hierarchical data multiple formats (Newick strings/files, data frames, lineage files, node-parent files), computes rectangle coordinates node, produces standard ggplot2 objects. pure-R reimplementation ggsunburst eliminates Python/reticulate dependency. Status: MVP complete (v0.1.0). Post-MVP roadmap SPEC.md §9. License: MIT R version: >= 4.1.0 (base pipe |>)

title: ggsunburstR
text: internal functions (exported) live R/tree-*.R, R/compute-*.R, R/parse-*.R, R/detect-input.R, R/utils.R. Exported functions: sunburst_data(), sunburst(), icicle(), plus S3 methods R/sunburst-class.R.
code: User API:     sunburst_data() → sunburst() / icicle() S3 class:     sunburst_data (print, plot, as.data.frame, $data alias) Computation:  assign_sizes → compute_coordinates → compute_label_positions Parsing:      detect_input_type → parse_newick / parse_dataframe / parse_lineage / parse_node_parent Tree:         new_tree, add_child, get_descendants, get_leaves, ladderize_tree, convert_to_ultrametric

title: ggsunburstR
text: Use devtools development workflows. Dependencies: ape, cli, ggplot2, rlang (Imports); testthat, vdiffr, knitr, rmarkdown (Suggests).

title: ggsunburstR
code: devtools::load_all()      # Load package for interactive use devtools::test()          # Run all 342 tests devtools::test(filter = "parse-newick")  # Run specific test file devtools::check()         # R CMD check (target: 0/0/0) devtools::document()      # Regenerate NAMESPACE and man/ from roxygen2 pkgdown::build_site()     # Build documentation site locally

title: ggsunburstR
text: Follow Tidyverse style guide. Use roxygen2 exported function documentation. Internal functions get code comments (# ...), roxygen2. Use testthat edition 3. Test files mirror source: R/parse-newick.R → tests/testthat/test-parse-newick.R. Pipe operator: use |> (base R pipe). Assignment: use <-, never =. Use rlang::abort() / warn() / inform() user-facing messages, never stop() / warning() / message(). Use :: one-calls dependencies. @importFrom ggsunburstR-package.R rlang stats. Internal functions: prefixed . truly private helpers (e.g., .resolve_values()), unprefixed internal--testable functions (e.g., parse_newick()).

title: ggsunburstR
text: Internal tree list (nodes, children, parent, root, n_tips) — exported, integer-indexed node IDs. See SPEC.md §2.1. add_child() copy semantics — returns modified tree via attr(, "tree") workaround R’s copy--modify. Pre-computed coordinates + geom_rect() — custom geom/stat ggproto (deferred post-MVP). fill string column name — tidy eval, simplicity MVP.

title: ggsunburstR
text: Branch main change: change/<slug> Commit messages: conventional commits (feat:, fix:, test:, docs:, chore:, refactor:) Never commit directly main — merge change branches review. OpenSpec manages change lifecycle openspec/ directory.

title: ggsunburstR
text: R CMD check: .github/workflows/R-CMD-check.yaml — runs macOS, Windows, Ubuntu (R devel/release/oldrel) pkgdown: .github/workflows/pkgdown.yaml — builds deploys gh-pages push main Site: https://anttirask.github.io/ggsunburstR/

title: Getting started with ggsunburstR
text: ggsunburstR creates sunburst icicle plots hierarchical data using ggplot2. accepts multiple input formats produces standard ggplot2 objects can customise freely.

title: Getting started with ggsunburstR
text: workflow two steps: Prepare data sunburst_data() Plot sunburst() icicle()
code: library(ggsunburstR)  # Parse a Newick tree string sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b, c), (d, e, f));")  # Sunburst plot sunburst(sb, fill = "depth") # Same data as an icicle plot icicle(sb, fill = "depth")

title: Getting started with ggsunburstR
text: common input phylogenetic hierarchical trees:
code: sb <- sunburst_data("((mammals, birds, reptiles)vertebrates, (beetles, flies)insects)animals;") sunburst(sb, fill = "name")

title: Getting started with ggsunburstR
text: Use data frame parent child columns:
code: df <- data.frame(   parent = c(NA, "root", "root", "fruits", "fruits", "vegs", "vegs"),   child  = c("root", "fruits", "vegs", "apple", "banana", "carrot", "pea") ) sb <- sunburst_data(df) sunburst(sb, fill = "name")

title: Getting started with ggsunburstR
text: ggsunburstR can read Newick files, node-parent CSVs, lineage TSVs directly passing file path.

title: Getting started with ggsunburstR
text: default, leaves get equal angular width. Use values size sectors proportionally:
code: sb <- sunburst_data(   "((a, b, c), (d, e));",   values = c(a = 10, b = 5, c = 3, d = 8, e = 2) ) sunburst(sb, fill = "name")

title: Getting started with ggsunburstR
text: Since output standard ggplot2 object, can add ggplot2 layer theme:
code: sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b, c), (d, e, f));") sunburst(sb, fill = "name") +   ggplot2::scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set2") +   ggplot2::labs(title = "Custom sunburst") +   ggplot2::theme(legend.position = "bottom")

title: Getting started with ggsunburstR
text: Add leaf labels show_labels = TRUE:
code: sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b, c), (d, e, f));") sunburst(sb, fill = "depth", show_labels = TRUE)

title: Getting started with ggsunburstR
text: Use label_type = "perpendicular" arc-following labels:
code: sunburst(sb, fill = "depth", show_labels = TRUE,          label_type = "perpendicular")

title: Getting started with ggsunburstR
text: Show labels internal nodes filter labels narrow sectors:
code: sb <- sunburst_data("((mammals, birds, reptiles)vertebrates, (beetles, flies)insects)animals;") sunburst(sb, fill = "name", show_labels = TRUE,          show_node_labels = TRUE, min_label_angle = 30,          label_size = 3.5)

title: Getting started with ggsunburstR
text: Use ggrepel collision avoidance icicle plots:
code: sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b, c, d, e), (f, g, h));") icicle(sb, fill = "depth", show_labels = TRUE, label_repel = TRUE)

title: Getting started with ggsunburstR
text: Use sunburst_multifill() icicle_multifill() map different colour scales different hierarchy levels:
code: sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b)X, (c, d)Y)root;") sunburst_multifill(sb, fills = list("1" = "name", "2" = "name"))

title: Getting started with ggsunburstR
text: Key parameters sunburst_data(): values — named numeric vector column name sector sizing branchvalues — "remainder" (default) "total" leaf_mode — "actual" (default) "extended" ladderize — sort descendant count ultrametric — equalise leaf depths xlim — angular span (default 360) rot — rotation offset degrees

title: Authors and Citation
text: Antti Rask. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation

what: Sunburst and Icicle Adjacency Diagrams with ggplot2
title: Sunburst and Icicle Adjacency Diagrams with ggplot2
text: Create sunburst, icicle, donut, tree adjacency diagrams using ggplot2. Accepts hierarchical data multiple formats, computes rectangle coordinates node, produces standard ggplot2 objects can customise familiar + syntax. pure-R reimplementation ggsunburst eliminates Python/reticulate dependency.

title: Sunburst and Icicle Adjacency Diagrams with ggplot2
text: Install development version GitHub:
code: # install.packages("pak") pak::pak("AnttiRask/ggsunburstR")

title: Sunburst and Icicle Adjacency Diagrams with ggplot2
code: library(ggsunburstR)  # From a Newick string sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b, c), (d, e, f));")  # Sunburst (polar) plot sunburst(sb, fill = "depth")  # Icicle (rectangular) plot icicle(sb, fill = "depth")  # Donut (ring) chart donut(sb, fill = "name")  # Tree (dendrogram) ggtree(sb)

title: Sunburst and Icicle Adjacency Diagrams with ggplot2
text: ggsunburstR accepts seven input formats:
code: # 1. Newick string sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b, c), (d, e, f));")  # 2. Newick file sb <- sunburst_data("path/to/tree.nw")  # 3. Data frame with parent-child columns df <- data.frame(   parent = c(NA, "root", "root", "A", "A"),   child  = c("root", "A", "B", "a1", "a2") ) sb <- sunburst_data(df)  # 4. Path-delimited strings sb <- sunburst_data(c("A/B/C", "A/B/D", "A/E"))  # 5. Data frame with path column sb <- sunburst_data(data.frame(path = c("A/B/C", "A/B/D")))  # 6. ape::phylo object phylo <- ape::read.tree(text = "(A, B, C);") sb <- sunburst_data(phylo)  # 7. data.tree::Node object root <- data.tree::Node$new("root") root$AddChild("A") root$AddChild("B") sb <- sunburst_data(root)

title: Sunburst and Icicle Adjacency Diagrams with ggplot2
text: Use values parameter size sectors data:
code: sb <- sunburst_data(   "((a, b, c), (d, e, f));",   values = c(a = 10, b = 5, c = 3, d = 8, e = 2, f = 1) ) sunburst(sb, fill = "name")

title: Sunburst and Icicle Adjacency Diagrams with ggplot2
text: Zoom specific branch:
code: sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b)X, (c, d)Y)root;") sub <- drilldown(sb, node = "X") sunburst(sub, fill = "name")

title: Sunburst and Icicle Adjacency Diagrams with ggplot2
text: Add bar charts heatmap tiles adjacent leaf nodes:
code: df <- data.frame(   parent = c(NA, "root", "root"),   child  = c("root", "A", "B"),   score  = c(NA, 0.5, 0.9),   group  = c(NA, "x", "y") ) sb <- sunburst_data(df) p <- icicle(sb, fill = "depth")  # Bar annotations bars(p, sb, variables = "score")  # Tile (heatmap) annotations tile(p, sb, variables = "group")

title: Sunburst and Icicle Adjacency Diagrams with ggplot2
text: Control label appearance sunburst icicle plots:
code: sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b, c), (d, e));")  # Arc-following (perpendicular) labels sunburst(sb, fill = "depth", show_labels = TRUE,          label_type = "perpendicular")  # Internal node labels + minimum angle filter sunburst(sb, fill = "depth", show_labels = TRUE,          show_node_labels = TRUE, min_label_angle = 30)  # ggrepel collision avoidance (icicle only) icicle(sb, fill = "depth", show_labels = TRUE, label_repel = TRUE)

title: Sunburst and Icicle Adjacency Diagrams with ggplot2
text: Use different colour scales different hierarchy levels:
code: sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b)X, (c, d)Y)root;")  # Different fill column per depth (requires ggnewscale) sunburst_multifill(sb, fills = list("1" = "name", "2" = "name")) icicle_multifill(sb, fills = list("1" = "name", "2" = "name"))

title: Sunburst and Icicle Adjacency Diagrams with ggplot2
code: sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b, c), (d, e));") p <- sunburst(sb, fill = "depth") highlight_nodes(p, nodes = c("a", "c"), fill = "red")

title: Sunburst and Icicle Adjacency Diagrams with ggplot2
text: output standard ggplot2 object:
code: sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b, c), (d, e, f));")  sunburst(sb, fill = "name") +   ggplot2::scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3") +   ggplot2::labs(title = "My Sunburst")

title: Sunburst and Icicle Adjacency Diagrams with ggplot2
text: MIT

title: Convert sunburst_data to data.frame — as.data.frame.sunburst_data
text: Returns rectangle data ($rects).

title: Convert sunburst_data to data.frame — as.data.frame.sunburst_data
code: # S3 method for class 'sunburst_data' as.data.frame(x, ...)

title: Convert sunburst_data to data.frame — as.data.frame.sunburst_data
text: x sunburst_data object. ... Ignored.

title: Convert sunburst_data to data.frame — as.data.frame.sunburst_data
text: data.frame containing rectangle coordinates.

what: Add bar chart annotations to a sunburst or icicle plot — bars
title: Add bar chart annotations to a sunburst or icicle plot — bars
text: Overlays bar charts adjacent leaf nodes, bar represents quantitative variable node attributes. Values max-normalised per variable 0–1 range.

title: Add bar chart annotations to a sunburst or icicle plot — bars
code: bars(   p,   sb,   variables,   y_offset = 0,   bar_height = 1,   box_colour = "black",   bar_colour = "black",   show_labels = FALSE,   show_values = FALSE,   label_size = 3,   value_size = 2.5,   ... )

title: Add bar chart annotations to a sunburst or icicle plot — bars
text: p ggplot object sunburst(), icicle(), ggtree(). sb sunburst_data object used create p. variables Character vector numeric column names sb$rects display bars. y_offset Vertical offset outermost ring. Default 0. bar_height Height bar band. Default 1. box_colour Outline colour outer box. Default "black". bar_colour Fill colour inner value bar. Default "black". show_labels Whether display variable names. Default FALSE. show_values Whether display numeric values inside bars. Default FALSE. label_size Text size variable labels. Default 3. value_size Text size value labels. Default 2.5. ... Passed outer geom_rect().

title: Add bar chart annotations to a sunburst or icicle plot — bars
text: input ggplot additional bar layers.

title: Add bar chart annotations to a sunburst or icicle plot — bars
code: df <- data.frame(   parent = c(NA, "root", "root"),   child  = c("root", "A", "B"),   score  = c(NA, 0.5, 0.9) ) sb <- sunburst_data(df) p <- icicle(sb, fill = "depth") bars(p, sb, variables = "score")

title: Access sunburst_data components — $.sunburst_data
text: $data accessor alias $rects.

title: Access sunburst_data components — $.sunburst_data
code: # S3 method for class 'sunburst_data' x$name

title: Access sunburst_data components — $.sunburst_data
text: x sunburst_data object. name Component name.

title: Create a donut (ring) chart — donut
text: Creates donut chart – sunburst restricted outermost 1–N depth levels. centre hole created shifting Y coordinates innermost displayed ring ymin > 0.

title: Create a donut (ring) chart — donut
code: donut(   sb,   levels = 1,   fill = NULL,   colour = "white",   linewidth = 0.2,   show_labels = FALSE,   hole_size = 1,   ... )

title: Create a donut (ring) chart — donut
text: sb sunburst_data object sunburst_data(). levels Number depth levels display (outermost inward). 1 = single ring, 2 = two concentric rings. fill Column name map fill aesthetic. NULL static grey. colour Border colour segments. Default "white". linewidth Border line width. Default 0.2. show_labels Whether display labels. Default FALSE. hole_size Size centre hole. Higher = larger hole relative ring thickness. Default 1. ... Passed geom_rect().

title: Create a donut (ring) chart — donut
text: ggplot object coord_polar() theme_void().

title: Create a donut (ring) chart — donut
code: sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b, c), (d, e));") donut(sb, fill = "name")  donut(sb, levels = 2, fill = "depth")

what: Drill down into a subtree — drilldown
title: Drill down into a subtree — drilldown
text: Extracts subtree rooted specified node recomputes coordinates subtree fills full angular space. result new sunburst_data object compatible plot functions.

title: Drill down into a subtree — drilldown
code: drilldown(sb, node, ...)

title: Drill down into a subtree — drilldown
text: sb sunburst_data object. node Node use new root. Character string (node name) integer (node ID). ... Override parameters recomputation (e.g., xlim, rot).

title: Drill down into a subtree — drilldown
text: new sunburst_data object rooted selected node.

title: Drill down into a subtree — drilldown
code: sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b)X, (c, d)Y)root;") # Drill into the X subtree (containing a, b) sub <- drilldown(sb, node = "X") sunburst(sub, fill = "name")

what: ggsunburstR: Sunburst and Icicle Adjacency Diagrams with ggplot2 — ggsunburstR-package
title: ggsunburstR: Sunburst and Icicle Adjacency Diagrams with ggplot2 — ggsunburstR-package
text: Create sunburst icicle adjacency diagrams using ggplot2. Accepts hierarchical data multiple formats (Newick strings/files, data frames, lineage files, node-parent files), computes rectangle coordinates node, produces standard ggplot2 objects users can customise familiar '+' syntax. pure-R reimplementation 'ggsunburst' eliminates Python/reticulate dependency.

title: ggsunburstR: Sunburst and Icicle Adjacency Diagrams with ggplot2 — ggsunburstR-package
text: Maintainer: Antti Rask antti@rask.fi

what: Create a tree-style dendrogram plot — ggtree
title: Create a tree-style dendrogram plot — ggtree
text: Creates classical node-link tree diagram (dendrogram) using geom_segment() segment data sunburst_data object.

title: Create a tree-style dendrogram plot — ggtree
code: ggtree(   sb,   colour = "black",   linewidth = 0.5,   show_labels = TRUE,   label_size = 3,   label_colour = "black",   rotate = TRUE,   polar = FALSE,   blank = TRUE,   show_scale = FALSE,   scale_length = 0,   ... )

title: Create a tree-style dendrogram plot — ggtree
text: sb sunburst_data object sunburst_data(). colour Line colour tree segments. Default "black". linewidth Line width tree segments. Default 0.5. show_labels Whether display leaf labels. Default TRUE. label_size Text size leaf labels. Default 3. label_colour Text colour leaf labels. Default "black". rotate TRUE (default), apply coord_flip() horizontal layout (root left, leaves right). polar TRUE, apply coord_polar() circular layout. Overrides rotate. blank TRUE (default), apply theme_void(). show_scale TRUE, display scale bar indicating branch-length units. Default FALSE. scale_length Length scale bar. 0 (default), auto-computed one tenth total tree depth. ... Passed geom_segment().

title: Create a tree-style dendrogram plot — ggtree
text: ggplot object.

title: Create a tree-style dendrogram plot — ggtree
text: Bioconductor package ggtree also exports ggtree() function. packages loaded, use ggsunburstR::ggtree() disambiguate.

title: Create a tree-style dendrogram plot — ggtree
code: sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b, c), (d, e));") ggtree(sb)  ggtree(sb, polar = TRUE)

what: Highlight specific nodes in a sunburst or icicle plot — highlight_nodes
title: Highlight specific nodes in a sunburst or icicle plot — highlight_nodes
text: Adds geom_rect() layer top existing plot visually emphasise specific nodes. Works sunburst() icicle() plots.

title: Highlight specific nodes in a sunburst or icicle plot — highlight_nodes
code: highlight_nodes(p, nodes, fill = "gold", colour = "black", linewidth = 0.5)

title: Highlight specific nodes in a sunburst or icicle plot — highlight_nodes
text: p ggplot object produced sunburst() icicle(). nodes Character vector node names integer vector node IDs highlight. fill Fill colour highlighted nodes. Default "gold". colour Border colour highlighted nodes. Default "black". linewidth Border line width highlighted nodes. Default 0.5.

title: Highlight specific nodes in a sunburst or icicle plot — highlight_nodes
text: input ggplot object additional highlight layer.

title: Highlight specific nodes in a sunburst or icicle plot — highlight_nodes
code: sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b, c), (d, e));") p <- sunburst(sb, fill = "depth") highlight_nodes(p, nodes = c("a", "c"), fill = "red")

what: Create a rectangular icicle plot — icicle
title: Create a rectangular icicle plot — icicle
text: Creates icicle (rectangular, top-) plot sunburst_data object using ggplot2::geom_rect() scale_y_reverse() theme_void().

title: Create a rectangular icicle plot — icicle
code: icicle(   sb,   fill = NULL,   colour = "white",   linewidth = 0.2,   show_labels = FALSE,   show_node_labels = FALSE,   label_size = 3,   min_label_angle = 0,   label_repel = FALSE,   ... )

title: Create a rectangular icicle plot — icicle
text: sb sunburst_data object sunburst_data(). fill Column name sb$rects map fill aesthetic. NULL, static grey fill used. colour Border colour rectangles. Default "white". linewidth Border line width. Default 0.2. show_labels Whether add text labels. Default FALSE. show_node_labels Whether add text labels internal nodes. takes effect show_labels = TRUE. Default FALSE. label_size Text size labels. Default 3. min_label_angle Minimum angular extent (degrees) node receive label. Nodes delta_angle < min_label_angle labelled. Default 0 (filtering). label_repel Use ggrepel::geom_text_repel() collision avoidance. Requires ggrepel package. Default FALSE. ... Passed geom_rect().

title: Create a rectangular icicle plot — icicle
text: ggplot object scale_y_reverse() theme_void().

title: Create a rectangular icicle plot — icicle
code: sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b, c), (d, e));") icicle(sb)  icicle(sb, fill = "depth")

what: Create an icicle plot with per-depth fill scales — icicle_multifill
title: Create an icicle plot with per-depth fill scales — icicle_multifill
text: Creates icicle plot different depth levels can use different fill colour mappings. Uses ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() enable multiple fill scales single plot.

title: Create an icicle plot with per-depth fill scales — icicle_multifill
code: icicle_multifill(sb, fills, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.2, ...)

title: Create an icicle plot with per-depth fill scales — icicle_multifill
text: sb sunburst_data object sunburst_data(). fills named list mapping depth levels (character strings) column names sb$rects fill mapping. E.g., list("1" = "name", "2" = "value"). colour Border colour rectangles. Default "white". linewidth Border line width. Default 0.2. ... Passed ggplot2::geom_rect().

title: Create an icicle plot with per-depth fill scales — icicle_multifill
text: ggplot object scale_y_reverse() theme_void().

title: Create an icicle plot with per-depth fill scales — icicle_multifill
code: sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b, c), (d, e));") if (requireNamespace("ggnewscale", quietly = TRUE)) {   icicle_multifill(sb, fills = list("1" = "name", "2" = "name")) }

what: Print tree structure from Newick input — nw_print
title: Print tree structure from Newick input — nw_print
text: Displays text summary tree encoded Newick string file. Useful inspecting tree topology creating plots.

title: Print tree structure from Newick input — nw_print
code: nw_print(newick, ladderize = FALSE, ultrametric = FALSE)

title: Print tree structure from Newick input — nw_print
text: newick Newick string file path. ladderize FALSE, sort partitions descendant count. TRUE "right" ascending, "left" descending. ultrametric TRUE, convert ultrametric topology printing.

title: Print tree structure from Newick input — nw_print
text: NULL, invisibly. Called side effect printing.

title: Print tree structure from Newick input — nw_print
code: nw_print("((a, b, c), (d, e));") #>  #> Phylogenetic tree with 5 tips and 3 internal nodes. #>  #> Tip labels: #>   a, b, c, d, e #>  #> Rooted; no branch length.

what: Create a polar sunburst plot — sunburst
title: Create a polar sunburst plot — sunburst
text: Creates sunburst (radial) plot sunburst_data object using ggplot2::geom_rect() coord_polar() theme_void().

title: Create a polar sunburst plot — sunburst
code: sunburst(   sb,   fill = NULL,   colour = "white",   linewidth = 0.2,   show_labels = FALSE,   show_node_labels = FALSE,   label_type = c("radial", "perpendicular"),   label_size = 3,   min_label_angle = 0,   label_repel = FALSE,   ... )

title: Create a polar sunburst plot — sunburst
text: sb sunburst_data object sunburst_data(). fill Column name sb$rects map fill aesthetic. NULL, static grey fill used. colour Border colour rectangles. Default "white". linewidth Border line width. Default 0.2. show_labels Whether add text labels leaf nodes. Default FALSE. show_node_labels Whether add text labels internal nodes. takes effect show_labels = TRUE. Default FALSE. label_type Label orientation. "radial": text reads outward. "perpendicular": text follows arc. label_size Text size labels. Default 3. min_label_angle Minimum angular extent (degrees) node receive label. Nodes delta_angle < min_label_angle labelled. Default 0 (filtering). label_repel supported sunburst plots. Use icicle() label repulsion, min_label_angle reduce label clutter sunbursts. Default FALSE. ... Passed geom_rect().

title: Create a polar sunburst plot — sunburst
text: ggplot object coord_polar() theme_void().

title: Create a polar sunburst plot — sunburst
code: sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b, c), (d, e));") sunburst(sb)  sunburst(sb, fill = "depth")  sunburst(sb, show_labels = TRUE, label_type = "perpendicular")

what: Parse hierarchical input into sunburst/icicle data — sunburst_data
title: Parse hierarchical input into sunburst/icicle data — sunburst_data
text: Accepts hierarchical data multiple formats (Newick strings/files, data frames, lineage files, node-parent files), computes rectangle coordinates node, returns sunburst_data S3 object suitable rendering sunburst() icicle().

title: Parse hierarchical input into sunburst/icicle data — sunburst_data
code: sunburst_data(   input,   type = "auto",   values = NULL,   branchvalues = c("remainder", "total"),   leaf_mode = c("actual", "extended"),   ladderize = FALSE,   ultrametric = FALSE,   xlim = 360,   rot = 0,   node_attributes = NULL,   sep = NULL,   ... )

title: Parse hierarchical input into sunburst/icicle data — sunburst_data
text: input Hierarchical data. One : Newick string, file path, data.frame parent-child columns, ape::phylo object, data.tree::Node object, character vector delimited paths, data.frame path column. type Input type. One "auto", "newick", "phylo", "datatree", "paths", "lineage", "node_parent", "dataframe". Auto-detection recommended. values Column name (character, data.frame input) named numeric vector mapping node names values sector sizing. NULL equal weight. branchvalues parent values relate children. "remainder": parent value additive. "total": parent value equals sum children. leaf_mode short branches handled. "actual": stop real depth. "extended": extend max depth. ladderize Sort partitions descendant count. FALSE sorting, TRUE "right" ascending, "left" descending. ultrametric TRUE, convert tree ultrametric topology. xlim Angular span degrees. Default 360 full circle. rot Rotation offset degrees. node_attributes Character vector additional node attribute names include output. sep Separator file-based inputs. ... Reserved future parameters.

title: Parse hierarchical input into sunburst/icicle data — sunburst_data
text: S3 object class "sunburst_data" containing $rects, $leaf_labels, $node_labels, $segments, $tree.

title: Parse hierarchical input into sunburst/icicle data — sunburst_data
code: # Newick string sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b, c), (d, e));") sb #>  #> ── Sunburst data  #> • 7 nodes (5 leaves, 2 internal) #> • 2 depth levels #> • xlim = 360°, rot = 0°  # Data frame df <- data.frame(   parent = c(NA, "root", "root"),   child  = c("root", "A", "B") ) sb <- sunburst_data(df)

what: Create a sunburst plot with per-depth fill scales — sunburst_multifill
title: Create a sunburst plot with per-depth fill scales — sunburst_multifill
text: Creates sunburst plot different depth levels can use different fill colour mappings. Uses ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() enable multiple fill scales single plot.

title: Create a sunburst plot with per-depth fill scales — sunburst_multifill
code: sunburst_multifill(sb, fills, colour = "white", linewidth = 0.2, ...)

title: Create a sunburst plot with per-depth fill scales — sunburst_multifill
text: sb sunburst_data object sunburst_data(). fills named list mapping depth levels (character strings) column names sb$rects fill mapping. E.g., list("1" = "name", "2" = "value"). colour Border colour rectangles. Default "white". linewidth Border line width. Default 0.2. ... Passed ggplot2::geom_rect().

title: Create a sunburst plot with per-depth fill scales — sunburst_multifill
text: ggplot object coord_polar() theme_void(). Users can add scale_fill_*() calls plot control depth's colour palette.

title: Create a sunburst plot with per-depth fill scales — sunburst_multifill
code: sb <- sunburst_data("((a, b, c), (d, e));") if (requireNamespace("ggnewscale", quietly = TRUE)) {   sunburst_multifill(sb, fills = list("1" = "name", "2" = "name")) }

what: Add tile (heatmap) annotations to a sunburst or icicle plot — tile
title: Add tile (heatmap) annotations to a sunburst or icicle plot — tile
text: Overlays coloured tiles adjacent leaf nodes, tile represents categorical continuous variable node attributes. fill aesthetic mapped value, allowing users add + scale_fill_*() call.

title: Add tile (heatmap) annotations to a sunburst or icicle plot — tile
code: tile(   p,   sb,   variables,   y_offset = 0,   tile_height = 1,   tile_width = 1,   colour = "white",   linewidth = 0,   show_labels = FALSE,   label_angle = 90,   label_size = 3,   ... )

title: Add tile (heatmap) annotations to a sunburst or icicle plot — tile
text: p ggplot object sunburst(), icicle(), ggtree(). sb sunburst_data object used create p. variables Character vector column names sb$rects display tiles. y_offset Vertical offset outermost ring. Default 0. tile_height Height tile band. Default 1. tile_width Width tile. Default 1. colour Border colour tiles. Default "white". linewidth Border line width. Default 0. show_labels Whether display variable names. Default FALSE. label_angle Rotation angle variable labels. Default 90. label_size Text size variable labels. Default 3. ... Passed geom_tile().

title: Add tile (heatmap) annotations to a sunburst or icicle plot — tile
text: input ggplot additional geom_tile() layer.

title: Add tile (heatmap) annotations to a sunburst or icicle plot — tile
text: Since tile() maps fill values, conflict fill aesthetic base plot. Use base plot without fill mapping (e.g., icicle(sb)) ggnewscale::new_scale_fill() calling.

title: Add tile (heatmap) annotations to a sunburst or icicle plot — tile
code: df <- data.frame(   parent = c(NA, "root", "root"),   child  = c("root", "A", "B"),   score  = c(NA, 0.5, 0.9) ) sb <- sunburst_data(df) p <- icicle(sb) tile(p, sb, variables = "score")

title: ggsunburstR 0.4.0
text: sunburst() gains label_type = "perpendicular" arc-following labels use pre-computed pangle/pvjust values position text radial midpoint. sunburst() icicle() gain show_node_labels internal node labels, label_size controlling text size, min_label_angle filtering labels narrow sectors. icicle() gains label_repel = TRUE ggrepel-based collision avoidance. Requires ggrepel package (Suggests). Polar repulsion sunbursts deferred – use min_label_angle instead.

title: ggsunburstR 0.4.0
text: sunburst_multifill() icicle_multifill() create plots per-depth fill colour scales using ggnewscale::new_scale_fill(). Depths listed fills rendered static grey.

title: ggsunburstR 0.4.0
text: sunburst_data() now includes ymin, ymax, delta_angle columns $leaf_labels data.frame, enabling perpendicular label positioning angular filtering.

title: ggsunburstR 0.4.0
text: Added ggrepel ggnewscale Suggests.

title: ggsunburstR 0.4.0
text: Added 69 new tests (569 total). R CMD check: 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes.

title: ggsunburstR 0.3.0
text: donut() creates ring charts – sunbursts restricted outermost 1–N depth levels configurable centre hole size. ggtree() creates classical dendrograms using geom_segment(). Supports horizontal layout (rotate = TRUE), circular layout (polar = TRUE), leaf labels, scale bars.

title: ggsunburstR 0.3.0
text: drilldown() extracts subtree recomputes coordinates selected node fills full angular space. Returns new sunburst_data object compatible plot functions. Supports chaining. bars() adds bar chart annotations adjacent leaf nodes. Values max-normalised per variable. Supports multiple variables, labels, value display. tile() adds heatmap-style tile annotations adjacent leaf nodes. Works numeric categorical variables.

title: ggsunburstR 0.3.0
text: Added 76 new tests (499 total). R CMD check: 0 errors, 0 warnings, 0 notes.

title: ggsunburstR 0.2.0
text: Accept ape::phylo objects directly – Newick conversion needed (sunburst_data(phylo_obj)). Path-delimited strings – sunburst_data(c("/B/C", "/B/D")) configurable separator via sep. Path-column data.frames – sunburst_data(data.frame(path = ...)) extra columns carried node attributes. data.tree::Node objects – R6-based trees custom scalar fields preserved attributes.

title: ggsunburstR 0.2.0
text: highlight_nodes() adds highlight layer sunburst/icicle plots specific nodes name ID. nw_print() prints tree structure Newick input inspection.

title: ggsunburstR 0.2.0
text: Fixed rbind column mismatch nodes varying extra columns different input sources.

title: ggsunburstR 0.2.0
text: Added 81 new tests (423 total), including comprehensive ragged tree edge-case coverage.

title: ggsunburstR 0.1.0
text: Initial release.

title: ggsunburstR 0.1.0
text: sunburst_data() parses hierarchical data 4 input formats: Newick strings/files, data.frames parent-child columns, lineage files, node-parent CSV/TSV files. sunburst() creates polar sunburst plots coord_polar(). icicle() creates rectangular icicle plots scale_y_reverse(). Value-weighted sectors via values parameter. Label support pre-computed positions flip logic readability. Tree transforms: ladderize ultrametric options. S3 class sunburst_data print(), plot(), .data.frame(), $data alias. Standard ggplot2 objects – customise +. 342 tests, R CMD check clean.
